title: Thinking About Converting? What You Need to Know First

sources.title: How to Convert to Judaism - What to Expect at a Conversion
sources.author: Menachem Posner and Yehuda Shurpin
sources.key_content: Three requirements: kabbalat mitzvot, mikvah, circumcision; Biblical basis from Sinai; spiritual perspective on convert's soul

sources.title: How to Convert to Judaism
sources.author: Kylie Ora Lobell
sources.key_content: Step-by-step process, timeline (minimum 1 year), community requirements, alternative of Noahide laws

sources.title: Book of Ruth 1:16-17
sources.key_content: Ruth's declaration: 'Your people shall be my people, and your God my God' - most famous convert in Jewish history

sources.title: The Dragon's Yarmulke: Jewish Communities in China
sources.key_content: NOTE: Conversion of local Chinese people currently prohibited in China; local Chinese cannot participate in rituals unless married to a Jew

key_requirements: Connect with recognized beit din (court of 3 rabbis)
Study period: minimum 1 year, often longer
Must live within walking distance of Orthodox synagogue
Learn to observe Shabbat and kashrut
Final steps: beit din examination, mikvah immersion
Men: circumcision or hatafat dam brit
research_notes.timeline: Minimum 1 year, typical 1-3 years, sometimes longer. In Israel: 10 months (400-450 study hours)
challenges: High cost of living in Orthodox neighborhoods
Loneliness when entering without Jewish family
Finding marriage partner in small communities
research_notes.china_specific: Currently, conversion of local Chinese nationals is not permitted within China. Chinese nationals interested in conversion would need to pursue this in another country (Israel, USA, etc.) or online study leading to conversion elsewhere.
research_notes.alternative: Noahide Laws (7 commandments) offer path to meaningful relationship with God without conversion
